rationale: The two papers are closely aligned conceptually: the cited study examines how product-harm crises affect customer-based brand equity (moderated by prior expectations), while the citing study investigates how mass service crises affect perceived service quality over time. Given this overlap in crisis-impact mechanisms and outcomes, the 2000 JMKR article is plausibly a foundational/theory source rather than an incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
